LLM01:2025.name: Prompt Injection
LLM01:2025.description: User or embedded content manipulates the model's behavior to make it perform unintended actions through its tools.

LLM02:2025.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
LLM02:2025.description: The model discloses sensitive data (secrets, PII, proprietary content) through its tool calls, including encoded exfiltration.

LLM03:2025.name: Supply Chain
LLM03:2025.description: Vulnerabilities in third-party models, datasets, and components introduced during build/training — a supply-chain concern outside a runtime tool-call PEP.

LLM04:2025.name: Data and Model Poisoning
LLM04:2025.description: Training or fine-tuning data is poisoned to introduce backdoors/bias. A model-lifecycle concern outside a runtime tool-call PEP.

LLM05:2025.name: Improper Output Handling
LLM05:2025.description: Downstream systems trust unsanitized model output; enforced by blocking dangerous tool calls (e.g. SQL injection, encoded payloads) that result from that output.

LLM06:2025.name: Excessive Agency
LLM06:2025.description: The agent has excessive permissions/autonomy and performs high-impact actions (e.g. shell/command execution) beyond its intent.

LLM07:2025.name: System Prompt Leakage
LLM07:2025.description: The system/meta prompt is extracted via crafted inputs. Runtime-observable at the tool boundary but no dedicated rule is loaded yet.

LLM08:2025.name: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
LLM08:2025.description: Weaknesses in the RAG vector store / embedding pipeline. An infrastructure/data concern outside a runtime tool-call PEP.

LLM09:2025.name: Misinformation
LLM09:2025.description: The model produces false or misleading output. An output-quality/model concern a runtime tool-call PEP cannot adjudicate.

LLM10:2025.name: Unbounded Consumption
LLM10:2025.description: Uncontrolled resource/cost consumption (denial-of-wallet). Runtime-enforceable via rate/cost limits on tool calls, but no dedicated rule is loaded yet.
